headlines.title: Russian attacks kill three in Ukraine as Kyiv hits another oil refinery
headlines.snippet: Russian attacks in Ukraine have resulted in three fatalities, with Kyiv reporting its own drone strikes hitting another oil refinery inside Russia. President Zelenskyy indicated that Ukraine is expanding its long-range drone campaign to strike deeper into Russian territory.

headlines.title: Venezuelan sky turns deep red as sun sets over Caracas
headlines.snippet: A striking sunset over Caracas, Venezuela, caused the sky to turn a deep red hue. This natural phenomenon captivated observers in the Venezuelan capital.

headlines.title: US-Iran negotiations: What’s the latest?
headlines.snippet: Iranian officials are anticipated to arrive in Doha for discussions with Qatari mediators. This comes a day after US negotiators reached Doha for similar talks.

headlines.title: Rescuers race to reach survivors in Venezuela
headlines.snippet: Rescuers are urgently working to reach survivors in Venezuela following an unspecified incident. One individual expressed a desperate plea for assistance in rescuing someone.

headlines.title: Trump official says White House will fight birthright citizenship decision
headlines.snippet: A White House adviser stated that the Trump administration intends to continue its efforts to end birthright citizenship. The adviser communicated this stance to Al Jazeera.

headlines.title: Starmer's legacy: Labour has a mountain to climb to recover its Muslim voters
headlines.snippet: The Labour party faces a significant challenge in regaining its Muslim voters following its stance on Gaza. This position caused a substantial rift, leading to the desertion of nearly half of its former voters, particularly in areas with large Muslim populations. The article references Prime Minister Keir Starmer and an interview from October 2023.

headlines.title: Iran-US indirect talks continue in Doha
headlines.snippet: Indirect negotiations between Iran and the United States are ongoing in Doha, according to a senior Iranian official. The discussions, which began Tuesday night and continued Wednesday, are reportedly focusing on the release of Iranian funds and issues related to the Strait of Hormuz. Qatar and Pakistan are serving as intermediaries in these talks.

headlines.title: Airlines told to avoid Iraq and Lebanon airspace
headlines.snippet: The European Union Aviation Safety Agency has advised airlines to avoid airspace over Iraq and Lebanon due to uncertainty surrounding the US-Iran ceasefire and the potential for rapid escalation. EASA has extended its conflict-zone advisory for the region until July 8.

headlines.title: Iran pushes for formal control over Strait of Hormuz
headlines.snippet: Iran is seeking international recognition for its control over the Strait of Hormuz, aiming to impose charges on vessels passing through. While an interim agreement with the United States allows uncharged passage for 60 days, Tehran asserts its authority over vessel access.

headlines.title: Former Israeli general says Gaza war could have ended earlier
headlines.snippet: A former head of the Israeli army's Hostages and Missing Persons Command stated that the war on Gaza was prolonged unnecessarily. Major General Nitzan Alon suggested that some captives could have been returned alive through earlier negotiations, and that similar strategic results could have been achieved with less duration.

headlines.title: Airlines should still avoid Iranian airspace, EU agency says
headlines.snippet: The European Union Aviation Safety Agency has advised airlines to continue avoiding Iranian airspace. This recommendation is in place for commercial air traffic.

headlines.title: Indirect US-Iran technical talks underway in Doha, source says
headlines.snippet: Indirect technical talks between the United States and Iran are currently taking place in Doha. The discussions are being facilitated by a source familiar with the situation.

headlines.title: Spain records more than 1,000 heat-related June deaths
headlines.snippet: Spain recorded over 1,000 deaths in June that were attributed to heat. This figure highlights the severe impact of the heatwave on the country during the month.

headlines.title: Iran was Unable to Export ’Single Barrel of Oil’ During Blockade
headlines.snippet: During a blockade, Iran was unable to export any oil. This statement indicates a complete cessation of oil exports for the duration of the blockade period.

headlines.title: Russia attacks retail fuel stations in Ukraine, killing one, official says
headlines.snippet: Russia has conducted attacks on retail fuel stations in Ukraine, resulting in one fatality, according to an official. The strikes targeted facilities supplying fuel within Ukraine.

headlines.title: How the Lebanon framework reshapes the US-Iran standoff
headlines.snippet: A deal involving the US, Israel, and Lebanon is seen as a broader strategy to undermine the US-Iran agreement and re-establish Washington's regional influence. This analysis suggests a geopolitical maneuvering behind the accord.

headlines.title: Trump weighed 'full-scale' return to war with Iran: report
headlines.snippet: Trump reportedly discussed the possibility of initiating large-scale strikes against Iran. Despite these considerations, diplomacy has been the chosen path, with indirect talks resuming in Doha.

headlines.title: In occupied southern Lebanon, Netanyahu vows Israel won't leave
headlines.snippet: In occupied southern Lebanon, Netanyahu declared that Israel will not withdraw. He made this statement during a visit to the area, accompanied by military officials.

headlines.title: Socialist and pro-Palestine Melat Kiros wins Colorado primary
headlines.snippet: Melat Kiros, a 29-year-old former attorney, has won a Colorado primary election. Kiros, who identifies as socialist and pro-Palestine, was previously dismissed from a law firm for criticizing its pro-Israel stance.

headlines.title: UN agency for Palestinian refugees nearing 'breaking point'
headlines.snippet: The UN agency for Palestinian refugees is approaching a 'breaking point.' Secretary-General Guterres warned that actions against the agency threatened the well-being of millions of Palestinians and its staff, noting that Israel had killed at least 390 UNRWA staffers.

headlines.title: إيران: أمين المجلس الأعلى للأمن القومي الإيراني: قبضة القائد الشهيد المرفوعة لحظة استشهاده هي رمز خالد لنهج أمننا القومي
headlines.snippet: The Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council of Iran stated that the raised fist of the martyred commander at the moment of his martyrdom is an eternal symbol of Iran's national security approach.

headlines.title: "وول ستريت جورنال": أُطلع ترامب على خيارات الحرب الشاملة مع إيران.. لكنه اختار التمسك بالمفاوضات
headlines.snippet: According to The Wall Street Journal, Trump was informed about options for a full-scale war with Iran but opted to pursue negotiations instead.

headlines.title: إيران: التلفزيون الإيراني: جنوح سفينة أجنبية في مضيق هرمز كانت تسلك مساراً غير المسار المحدد وفق التعليمات الإيرانية
headlines.snippet: Iranian television reported that a foreign vessel that ran aground in the Strait of Hormuz was not following the designated route according to Iranian instructions.

headlines.title: الأمين العام لحلف "الناتو": المعلومات التي تزودنا بها القيادة التركية تكتسي أهمية حاسمة في الحفاظ على أمن الحلف بأسره
headlines.snippet: The Secretary General of NATO stated that the information provided by the Turkish leadership is crucial for maintaining the security of the entire alliance.

headlines.title: إيران: التلفزيون الإيراني عن القائم بأعمال وزارة الدفاع الإيرانية: سيتم تطوير قدراتنا العسكرية بالاعتماد على القدرات المحلية
headlines.snippet: Iranian television cited the acting Iranian Minister of Defense, who said that Iran's military capabilities would be developed using domestic resources.

headlines.title: Over 32K tourists visit Iran’s Takht-e Soleyman World Heritage Site
headlines.snippet: Over 32,000 domestic and foreign tourists visited Iran's Takht-e Soleyman World Heritage Site since March 21, 2026. The director of the site announced these visitor numbers.

headlines.title: Iran’s nuclear chief calls for faster implementation of plasma-based wastewater treatment project
headlines.snippet: Mohammad Eslami, the head of Iran's Atomic Energy Organization, has called for the expedited implementation of a cold plasma-based industrial wastewater treatment project. He emphasized its potential environmental benefits.

headlines.title: Exclusive | Martyred Iranian Leader’s vision will endure across Muslim world: Lebanese scholar
headlines.snippet: A Lebanese scholar stated that the vision of the martyred Leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei, will continue to have an influence across the Muslim world. The scholar highlighted Khamenei's role in promoting Islamic unity and supporting resistance.

headlines.title: Iran’s envoy urges accountability over Western role in Sardasht chemical attack
headlines.snippet: Iran's ambassador to Germany has called for accountability from Western companies involved in the 1987 chemical attack on Sardasht. He argued that these companies provided the necessary materials and technology to Saddam Hussein's regime.

headlines.title: Iran, Belarus seek to expand agricultural cooperation
headlines.snippet: Iran and Belarus are seeking to enhance their agricultural cooperation and increase trade. The Iranian agriculture minister stressed the importance of accelerating their joint agricultural commission meetings.

headlines.title: Iran’s missile, UAV capabilities nonnegotiable: Defense min.
headlines.snippet: Iran's Defense Minister stated that the country's missile and UAV capabilities are non-negotiable. This firm stance was reported by Mehr News Agency.

headlines.title: FM Araghchi lashes out at US Sec.’s remarks over Iran at WC
headlines.snippet: FM Araghchi has criticized remarks made by the US Secretary concerning Iran at the World Cup. The comments were reported by Mehr News Agency.

headlines.title: US military deploys over 900 personnel to Venezuela
headlines.snippet: The US military has deployed over 900 personnel to Venezuela. This deployment was reported by Mehr News Agency.

headlines.title: UN chief urges nations to fund $100m UNRWA gap
headlines.snippet: The UN chief has urged nations to contribute to a $100 million funding gap for UNRWA. The appeal was reported by Mehr News Agency.

headlines.title: Pakistan says it intercepted four drones launched from Afghan
headlines.snippet: Pakistan reported intercepting four drones that were launched from Afghanistan. The incident was reported by Mehr News Agency.

headlines.title: Iranians barely visible in US-Iran deal, UN rapporteur says - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: A UN rapporteur has stated that Iranians are barely visible in the US-Iran deal. This observation was reported by Iran International.

headlines.title: IRGC moves to seize historic Protestant church in Tehran - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: The IRGC is reportedly moving to seize a historic Protestant church in Tehran. The report comes from Iran International.

headlines.title: Atlas of Iran’s Missile Cities - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: An 'Atlas of Iran’s Missile Cities' has been published. The information was reported by Iran International.

headlines.title: Khamenei to end Eje'i’s judiciary tenure after one term - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: Khamenei is expected to end Eje'i’s tenure as head of the judiciary after a single term. This information was reported by Iran International.

headlines.title: Deadly attacks shake northwest Iran as IRGC reports new clashes - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: Deadly attacks have occurred in northwest Iran, with the IRGC reporting new clashes. The events were reported by Iran International.

headlines.title: World Bank approves $700 million in financing to boost investment, jobs in Jordan
headlines.snippet: The World Bank has approved $700 million in financing for Jordan. This funding aims to boost investment and create jobs in the country.

headlines.title: In south Lebanon, Netanyahu says Israel will stay as long as Hizbollah 'threatens us'
headlines.snippet: Netanyahu stated that Israel will remain in southern Lebanon as long as Hezbollah poses a threat. He made this declaration during a visit to the area, joined by military officials.

headlines.title: Thousands expected as Vespa celebrates 80 years in Rome
headlines.snippet: Thousands are expected to gather in Rome as Vespa celebrates its 80th anniversary. The event will mark a significant milestone for the iconic brand.

headlines.title: RJ following up on crew bus collision in New York
headlines.snippet: RJ is following up on a crew bus collision that occurred in New York. The details of the incident are under investigation.

headlines.title: GDP rises to 2.9% in Q1 2026, up from 2.7% in 2025 — DoS
headlines.snippet: The GDP rose to 2.9% in the first quarter of 2026, an increase from 2.7% in 2025. This data was released by the Department of Statistics.

headlines.title: Katz: Katz: Israel will remain in Lebanon, Syria, and Gaza ‘indefinitely'; Israel establishes 2 new military checkpoints near Naqoura, southern Lebanon
headlines.snippet: Katz stated that Israel intends to remain in Lebanon, Syria, and Gaza indefinitely. Additionally, Israel has established two new military checkpoints near Naqoura in southern Lebanon.

headlines.title: Passengers delighted to ride UAE's first ever passenger train
headlines.snippet: Passengers expressed delight in riding the UAE's first-ever passenger train. The new service marks a significant development in the region's transportation network.

headlines.title: Dangerous heat wave to grip US as nation marks 250 years
headlines.snippet: A severe heat wave is predicted to impact the US as the nation prepares to mark its 250th anniversary. The forecast indicates challenging weather conditions.

headlines.title: Financial gap: BDL revises government plan, leaving depositors to wait longer
headlines.snippet: A financial gap has led to a revision of the government's plan, which will cause depositors to wait longer. The details of the revision were reported by L'Orient Today.

headlines.title: 'Campaigns targeting the army and its leadership will not affect its action,' Aoun says
headlines.snippet: Aoun stated that campaigns targeting the army and its leadership will not impede its actions. He asserted the army's resolve.

headlines.title: EgyptAir launches first direct flights to Venice, Italy
headlines.snippet: EgyptAir launched its inaugural direct flights to Venice, Italy, utilizing an Airbus A320neo aircraft. This expansion enhances the airline's European network.

headlines.title: Iran raises the stakes – no negotiations without these conditions
headlines.snippet: Iran's chief negotiator, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, emphasized that Tehran will not engage in new negotiations until stipulated conditions are met. He highlighted the ongoing efforts to implement an existing memorandum of understanding.

headlines.title: Ten electric buses to serve commuters in the New Administrative Capital
headlines.snippet: Egypt's Minister of Transport inaugurated ten electric buses for the New Administrative Capital. These buses are intended to support a smart, green public transport system.

headlines.title: Justice minister, German ambassador discuss judicial cooperation
headlines.snippet: Minister of Justice Mahmoud al-Sherif and German Ambassador Jürgen Schulz met to discuss judicial cooperation and capacity building. They reviewed existing collaborations and potential future legal partnerships.

headlines.title: Local Development Minister, Cairo Governor review waste management system
headlines.snippet: The Minister of Local Development and Environment and the Cairo Governor reviewed the city's integrated municipal solid waste management system. Discussions included waste collection, treatment, and disposal processes.

headlines.title: نتنياهو عن المساعدات المالية الأميركية: أشبه بالإعانة الاجتماعية... لا أريدها - Al Akhbar
headlines.snippet: Netanyahu described US financial aid as akin to 'social assistance' and stated, 'I don't want it.'

headlines.snippet: This item appears to be a placeholder or potentially an incomplete entry and does not contain a summaryable news item.

headlines.snippet: The Wall Street Journal reports that Trump is giving diplomacy an additional opportunity after Iran suspended negotiations.

headlines.title: إيران تعلّق المفاوضات: ملف هرمز وتحرير لبنان أولاً - Al Akhbar
headlines.snippet: Iran has suspended negotiations, prioritizing the issues of Hormuz and the liberation of Lebanon.

headlines.snippet: The military judiciary has concluded political detentions, indicating a shift in process where arrest precedes the determination of charges.

headlines.title: العاملون في الجامعة اللبنانية اعتصموا أمام السرايا و ولوحوا بالتصعيد إذا لم تُصرف مستحقاتهم
headlines.snippet: Workers at the Lebanese University staged a protest demanding payment of their dues, including a doubled salary and back pay since November 2023. They threatened further escalation if their demands are not met.

headlines.title: لابيد: علاقات “اسرائيل” الخارجية كارثية.. واستمرار تقديم الاعتبارات الحزبية على الاعتبارات السياسية “للدولة”
headlines.snippet: Yair Lapid described Israel's foreign relations as catastrophic and poorly managed. He criticized the prioritization of political considerations over state interests.

headlines.title: بعد قرار المحكمة العليا.. هل فشلت خطة ترمب لإلغاء “حق المواطنة بالولادة”؟
headlines.snippet: The Supreme Court's decision has dealt a blow to Trump's policies aimed at revoking 'birthright citizenship.' The court upheld the right to citizenship for nearly all individuals born in the United States.

headlines.title: الوزير جابر من الرباط: بناء المؤسسات هو الضمانة الحقيقية للإصلاح والشراكات المستدامة تُثبت قيمتها في أصعب الأزمات
headlines.snippet: Minister of Finance Yassin Jaber emphasized that strong institutions are the foundation for economic reforms. He highlighted the importance of investing in capacity building and fostering sustainable international partnerships.

headlines.title: شرطة العدو تزعم إحباط عملية طعن في اللد
headlines.snippet: Israeli police claim to have shot and neutralized an individual in Lod who allegedly attempted to stab a police officer. The suspect is believed to be an Arab not residing in the occupied Palestinian territories.

headlines.title: Colonists block roads, spray racist graffiti in Wadi Sa’ir, Hebron - WAFA Agency
headlines.snippet: Colonists blocked roads and spray-painted racist graffiti in Wadi Sa'ir, Hebron. This action was reported by the WAFA Agency.

headlines.title: OIC welcomes UN report on Israeli crimes against Palestinian children - WAFA Agency
headlines.snippet: The OIC has welcomed a UN report detailing Israeli actions against Palestinian children. The statement was issued by the WAFA Agency.

headlines.title: Israeli forces assault mourners at funeral in Jerusalem - WAFA Agency
headlines.snippet: Israeli forces reportedly assaulted mourners during a funeral in Jerusalem. The incident was reported by the WAFA Agency.

headlines.title: Colonists install caravans on Jabal al-Ras in Umm Safa, north of Ramallah - WAFA Agency
headlines.snippet: Colonists have installed caravans on Jabal al-Ras in Umm Safa, north of Ramallah. This activity was reported by the WAFA Agency.

headlines.title: Colonists establish new outpost on lands of Kafr Ra’i, southwest of Jenin - WAFA Agency
headlines.snippet: Colonists have established a new outpost on lands belonging to Kafr Ra'i, southwest of Jenin. The action was reported by the WAFA Agency.

headlines.snippet: Regular coffee consumption may reduce the risk of liver cancer and cirrhosis. This finding was reported by paltoday.ps.

headlines.snippet: Fuel and gas prices in Palestine have decreased. This price drop was reported by paltoday.ps.

headlines.snippet: Following a recent increase, the price of the dollar has seen a further decrease today, Wednesday. This information was reported by paltoday.ps.

headlines.snippet: Under the supervision of the World Health Organization, 30 patients were evacuated from Gaza via Rafah. This medical evacuation was reported by paltoday.ps.

headlines.snippet: The report titled 'A Thousand Days of Israeli Genocide and a Truce Under Fire' highlights a prolonged period of conflict and a fragile ceasefire. This was reported by paltoday.ps.

headlines.snippet: Amnesty International has stated that the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) committed ethnic cleansing in Sudan. The report was published by Arab News.

headlines.snippet: China's Xi urged the ruling Communist Party to remain adaptable and safeguard its achievements. This address was reported by Arab News.

headlines.title: ‘Riyadh is the closest thing to home,’ says newly crowned WWE champion Sami Zayn
headlines.snippet: Newly crowned WWE champion Sami Zayn described Riyadh as 'the closest thing to home.' The statement was made following his victory and reported by Arab News.

headlines.snippet: Three UN peacekeepers were wounded in an attack on a base in the Central African Republic. The incident was reported by Arab News.

headlines.snippet: Iran is making preparations for the funeral of its late supreme leader. The arrangements are underway, as reported by Arab News.

headlines.snippet: July's biggest K-drama releases include shows featuring Nam Joo-hyuk and Lee Dong-wook. This entertainment news was reported by Gulf News.

headlines.title: France says firms risk violating international law through business in illegal Israeli settlements
headlines.snippet: France has warned that companies conducting business in illegal Israeli settlements risk violating international law. The French Foreign Ministry highlighted potential legal, economic, and reputational risks.

headlines.title: Gobeklitepe draws 4.4M visitors in 8 years on UNESCO list
headlines.snippet: Gobeklitepe, a UNESCO World Heritage site in southeastern Türkiye, has attracted 4.4 million visitors in eight years. The 12,000-year-old archaeological site continues to draw global attention.

headlines.title: Israeli army blocks West Bank town, cuts all access roads
headlines.snippet: Israeli army forces have blocked a West Bank town, sealing all access roads. The mayor described the closure of Sinjil as an 'unprecedented escalation' of collective punishment.

headlines.title: Fewer babies born in Germany in nearly 30 years, report shows
headlines.snippet: Germany has recorded fewer births in nearly 30 years, intensifying concerns about its aging population. The report highlights a demographic trend.

headlines.title: Israeli opposition leader accuses Netanyahu of fabricating Iran nuclear claims to frighten public
headlines.snippet: An Israeli opposition leader accused Netanyahu of fabricating claims about Iran's nuclear program to frighten the public. Gadi Eisenkot stated that Iran does not possess nuclear bombs.

headlines.snippet: Intelligence chiefs from Türkiye and Egypt met in Ankara to discuss Gaza and regional security. The meeting was reported by Daily Sabah.

headlines.snippet: The article discusses Türkiye's expectations from the upcoming NATO summit in Ankara, focusing on the alliance's future direction. This was reported by Daily Sabah.

headlines.snippet: Turkish F-16 fighter jets escorted a flight after a false hijacking alert. The incident was reported by Daily Sabah.

headlines.snippet: Türkiye remains committed to its goal of EU membership, according to Foreign Minister Fidan. This statement was reported by Daily Sabah.

headlines.snippet: President Erdoğan stated that Türkiye is prepared for all scenarios. His remarks were reported by Daily Sabah.

headlines.snippet: The Kılıçdaroğlu-led CHP party has purged 26 provincial chiefs. This internal party development was reported by Hürriyet Daily News.

headlines.snippet: Türkiye is planning a military service exemption for citizens who have served abroad. This policy change was reported by Hürriyet Daily News.

headlines.snippet: A former Israeli army chief and an ex-prime minister have criticized Netanyahu's 'loss of direction.' Their critical remarks were reported by TRT World.

headlines.snippet: The Turkish intelligence chief met with his Egyptian counterpart in Ankara. This diplomatic engagement was reported by TRT World.

headlines.snippet: JD Vance commented that Iran's denial of Doha talks is a 'Persian negotiating tactic.' His statement was reported by TRT World.
